message:
fix(mcp): guard refresh-cooldown against i64 overflow + remove dead build_policy_engine helper

Two small hardening changes to the MCP surface.

## Cooldown arithmetic

`check_refresh_cooldown` was computing `now_ms - most_recent_refresh_ms`
with plain subtraction. For adversarial or clock-skewed callers the
subtraction could overflow:

  now_ms_val = i64::MAX
  most_recent_refresh_ms = i64::MIN
  => now - recent = panics in debug, wraps in release

Both copies of the helper — `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs` and
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_helpers.rs` — now do
`now_ms_val.saturating_sub(most_recent_refresh_ms).max(0)`. The `.max(0)`
keeps "future timestamp" callers (recent > now) from reporting a
negative elapsed — the clamp treats them as "just refreshed, cooldown
applies in full". The `mcp_helpers` version also picks up the
`cooldown_ms <= 0` short-circuit the `mcp.rs` copy already had, so a
zero or negative cooldown disables the check uniformly instead of
accidentally producing a Some(...) with a negative `remaining`.

Regression coverage (both files):
- `check_refresh_cooldown_extreme_future_timestamp_does_not_overflow`:
  `check_refresh_cooldown(i64::MAX, i64::MIN, 60_000)` — must return
  `Some((0, 60))` and not panic.
- `cooldown_future_timestamp_clamps_elapsed_to_zero` (mcp_helpers):
  recent > now returns `Some((0, 60))` rather than a negative elapsed.
- `cooldown_non_positive_window_is_disabled` (mcp_helpers): cooldown
  of 0 or -1 returns `None`.

## Dead helper

`mcp_helpers::build_policy_engine` is removed. After the shared
rate-limiter refactor in f540a5d5 the MCP server hands out a shared
`Arc<Mutex<RateLimiter>>`, and every tool handler now constructs its
engine via `build_policy_engine_with_shared_rate_limiter` (in
mcp.rs). The old fresh-per-request helper is a landmine for future
tool authors — grab-via-autocomplete resurrects the exact rate-limit
leak ft-eu0no fixed.

- Delete `pub(super) fn build_policy_engine(config, require_prompt)`
  from crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_helpers.rs.
- Prune `PolicyEngine` from the `use crate::policy::{...}` list since
  nothing else in the file still references it.

No call sites remain; removal is source-level only. Verified by
`rg '\bbuild_policy_engine\b' crates/` — every hit now resolves to
the shared-limiter variant in `mcp.rs`.
